nl.statement: design-based finite-population potential-outcome system on n=2m units: the only randomness is a sign assignment law P (a PMF on the finite space {-1,1}^n); potential outcomes Y_i(z) and the GATE estimand tau are fixed finite-population objects — FiniteDesign Ω is exactly a PMF (p, p_nonneg, p_sum) over a finite assignment space with finite-sum E/Var/Cov; instantiate Ω := (Fin n -> Sign) i.e. {-1,1}^n so P : FiniteDesign Ω. Sign-symmetry / block-exchangeability (P_m^bal, P_m^sym) are predicates ON such a design (built by BalancedDesignClass / blockExchangeableDesignClass nodes). Y_i(z):(Ω)->Fin n->R and tau=n^{-1} sum (Y_i(1_n)-Y_i(-1_n)) are plain ambient functions (no dedicated Causalean PO structure; lean_local_search PotentialOutcome -> none, only Panel.Exposure exists) and are motivational: no proved statement references them, so they are trivial Mathlib functions carried in this world.

real n x n symmetric-matrix / Euclidean objective-geometry world (Mathlib): traces, PSD cone, Frobenius (Schatten-2) norm, the balanced two-block spectral decomposition, and the two-parameter block-symmetric slice X(u,v) with its reduced triangle T_m — Built over Mathlib matrices: Matrix.trace, Matrix.PosSemidef, the Frobenius norm, EuclideanSpace/inner products and the spectral theorem for symmetric matrices supply every primitive. L_m^dagger (Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse) is only ever consumed through its eigenvalues (1/(a+b),1/(2b),0) via lem:block-spectral-coordinates, so it is realized by its spectral action, not a general pseudoinverse theory. bypass-justified: the Causalean analogue for the ambient probability layer (FiniteDesign, S1) is at the design-law abstraction, whereas E_m^blk/F/Delta are purely algebraic over R^{n x n}; lean_local_search {elliptope, assignmentCovariance, Laplacian, Frobenius} returned none, so this world is a Mathlib-matrix bypass. The derived scalar frontiers (kappa_cut, r_cut, kappa_iid, r_gap^{+-}, r_star) are R-valued closed forms living in this world; r_star has no statement node (honest open scope) and is bound here only as a symbol.
P(Z = z) = P(Z = -z) for all z in {-1,1}^n.
Vertex set A_m union B_m with |A_m|=|B_m|=m; for i != j in the same block set W_ij = a/m, for i and j in opposite blocks set W_ij = b/m, and set W_ii = 0.
{ P on {-1,1}^n : P(Z=z)=P(Z=-z) for every z }
{ P in P_m^bal : P is invariant under permutations within A_m, within B_m, and under swapping the two equal communities }
Tr(L_m X) + r Tr(L_m^dagger X) + kappa ||X||_{S_2} + Tr(J_n X)
inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) - inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X)
Law of (sum_{i in A_m} Z_i, sum_{i in B_m} Z_i) under P.

Assume ass:two-block-homophily, with r in [0,infty) and kappa in [0,infty) by their definitional domains. Orbit-averaging over the full two-block automorphism group generated by permutations within A_m, permutations within B_m, and the swap of the two equal communities maps each elliptope point X with diag(X)=1 to a point in E_m^blk with weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}. The same averaging maps each law P in P_m^bal to a block-exchangeable law with the same zero one-point margins and weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}(X(P)). Consequently inf_{X : X is positive semidefinite and diag(X)=1} F_{r,kappa}(X) = inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X) and inf_{P in P_m^bal} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) = inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)).

Assume ass:two-block-homophily. Set r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)=min{2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)), 2b(2m-2b-kappa)}. If 0<=r<r_cut(m,a,b,kappa), then X_cut is the unique minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk. Hence Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)=0, X_cut is the unique minimizer over C_m^pm, and P_cut attains that implementable optimum. (The range 0<=r<r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) is nonempty exactly when 0<=kappa<min{a-b,2(m-b)}, which in particular requires b<m; this is a non-vacuity remark, not a hypothesis.) At the boundary r=r_cut uniqueness can fail already when kappa=0, so the theorem uses the strict inequality r<r_cut.

Assume ass:two-block-homophily. Finite-kappa iid exactness holds only on the affine-balanced locus. More precisely, I_n is a minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk for some finite kappa if and only if a+3b=2m and r=2b(a+b), equivalently r=4b(m-b). On this locus, for every kappa>0, I_n is the unique relaxed minimizer and the implementable optimum is attained by P_iid, with P_iid in P_m^sym by def:block-exchangeable-design-class and X(P_iid)=I_n. Off this locus, I_n is not a minimizer for any finite kappa, although minimizers converge to I_n as kappa tends to infinity.

Assume ass:two-block-homophily, ass:low-scale-two-block, and ass:odd-community-size. With q=2(m-1), define kappa_gap(m,a,b):=((2m-a-3b)(a-b)sqrt(q))/(a+b) and r_cut_gap(m,a,b,kappa):=2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)). Define R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)=2b(a+b)(1+kappa/((a-b)sqrt(q))) and R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa)=(a+b)(2m-a-b-kappa/sqrt(q)). Define r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa)=(R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)+R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa))/2 and r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa)=R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa). Then for every 0 <= kappa < kappa_gap(m,a,b), r_cut_gap(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa), and for every r in (r_gap^-,r_gap^+) the spread vertex X_spread in E_m^blk with spectral coordinates (m/(m-1),0,0) is the unique relaxed minimizer but is not plus-minus-one implementable. Consequently Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)>0 throughout that interval. The oddness restriction is necessary: if m is even, the whole block elliptope slice is implementable and the claimed positive gap is false.

Under ass:two-block-homophily, with r,kappa in [0,infty), the theorem asserts EXACTLY the following conjunction (mirroring the Lean statement): (i) rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa) = Delta_m^pm(r,kappa); (ii) 0 <= rho_star and 0 <= Delta_m^pm (definitional domain [0,infty)); (iii) rho_star=0 iff there is a relaxed minimizer over T_m (a point of T_m minimizing the reduced objective) with y+z >= d_m; (iv) for kappa>0 the relaxed minimizer over T_m is UNIQUE, and rho_star=0 iff that unique minimizer satisfies y+z >= d_m; (v) for kappa=0 a point of T_m is a relaxed minimizer iff it lies on the exposed face of the alpha=(c_x/q,c_y,c_z)-minimizing coordinates; (vi) for even m (d_m=0) rho_star=0 throughout; (vii) ACTIVE-SET COMPUTATION (lem:weighted-simplex-active-set): for kappa>0 the unique relaxed minimizer is the active-set point of an admissible support/multiplier (S,lambda) under the change of variables t=(qx,y,z) (alpha=sharpAlpha, beta=sharpBeta), and the relaxed value equals 2m*lambda; (viii) TRUNCATION CORRECTION (lem:weighted-simplex-truncation): when the relaxed argmin set is disjoint from {y+z >= d_m}, the implementable value is attained on the truncation segment {y+z=d_m} at the endpoint/interior selector truncSelector. No r_star frontier/boundary is asserted (honest open scope).

Let q=2(m-1). For X(u,v), define x=1-u, y=1+(m-1)u-mv, and z=1+(m-1)u+mv. Then X(u,v) belongs to E_m^blk if and only if x,y,z>=0 and qx+y+z=2m. In these coordinates X_cut=(0,2m,0), I_n=(1,1,1), and F_{r,kappa}(X(u,v))=c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2), where c_x=q((a+b)+r/(a+b)), c_y=2b+r/(2b), and c_z=2m.
On T_m={x,y,z>=0: qx+y+z=2m}, if c_x/q>c_y+kappa and c_z>c_y+kappa, then (0,2m,0) is the unique minimizer of c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2).
On T_m={x,y,z>=0: qx+y+z=2m}, if c_y>c_x/q+kappa/sqrt(q) and c_z>c_x/q+kappa/sqrt(q), then (2m/q,0,0) is the unique minimizer of c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2).

On T_m, the point (1,1,1) is the unique minimizer of sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2). It is a minimizer of c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2) for finite kappa only if c_x/q=c_y=c_z; under this equality and kappa>0 it is the unique minimizer.

After full two-block symmetrization, the plus-minus-one covariance image in spectral coordinates is T_m when m is even and is T_m intersect {y+z>=2/m} when m is odd. In particular, for odd m the spread vertex (m/(m-1),0,0) is not implementable, while for even m every point of E_m^blk is implementable.

Let I={x,y,z}, M=2m, beta_x=1/q, beta_y=beta_z=1, and alpha=(alpha_x,alpha_y,alpha_z). On Delta_M={t_i>=0: sum_i t_i=M}, minimize Phi(t)=sum_i alpha_i t_i+kappa sqrt(sum_i beta_i t_i^2). If kappa>0, the minimizer is unique and is obtained by the following active-set formula. For each nonempty S subset I set A_S=sum_{i in S} beta_i^{-1}, mu_S=A_S^{-1} sum_{i in S} alpha_i beta_i^{-1}, and V_S=sum_{i in S}(alpha_i-mu_S)^2 beta_i^{-1}. If lambda_S=mu_S+sqrt((kappa^2-V_S)/A_S) is real, lambda_S>max_{i in S} alpha_i, and alpha_j>=lambda_S for every j notin S, then t_i=M((lambda_S-alpha_i)/beta_i)/sum_{h in S}((lambda_S-alpha_h)/beta_h) for i in S and t_j=0 for j notin S. Exactly one S satisfies these conditions, and the displayed point is the unique minimizer with value M lambda_S. Singleton S gives a vertex, two-point S gives an edge minimizer, and S=I gives the interior minimizer. If kappa=0, the minimizer set is the exposed face conv{M e_i: alpha_i=min_j alpha_j}; a closed-form selector may be chosen from that face without changing the value.

In the notation of lem:weighted-simplex-active-set, let K_d={t in Delta_M: t_y+t_z>=d} with 0<=d<=M. Use the kappa=0 tie convention that the relaxed selector is chosen in K_d when the relaxed argmin face meets K_d. If the selected relaxed minimizer t_rel belongs to K_d, it is also a minimizer over K_d. If t_rel is not in K_d, then every constrained optimum may be chosen on the segment H_d={t_x=M-d, t_y=s, t_z=d-s, 0<=s<=d}. On H_d the objective is g_d(s)=alpha_x(M-d)+alpha_z d+(alpha_y-alpha_z)s+kappa sqrt((M-d)^2/q+s^2+(d-s)^2). For kappa>0, writing A_d=(M-d)^2/q and delta=alpha_y-alpha_z, the minimizer is s=0 if delta>=kappa d/sqrt(A_d+d^2), s=d if delta<=-kappa d/sqrt(A_d+d^2), and otherwise s=(d-delta sqrt((A_d+d^2/2)/(kappa^2-delta^2/2)))/2. For kappa=0, any endpoint minimizing the linear function is selected: s=0 if delta>=0 and s=d if delta<=0.
nl.frozen_body:
Let \(m\ge 2\), let \(M,d\in\mathbb R\), and let \(\alpha=(\alpha_x,\alpha_y,\alpha_z)\), \(\beta=(\beta_x,\beta_y,\beta_z)\), and \(\kappa\in\mathbb R\). Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Mass and truncation.)} \(M>0\), \(M=2m\), and \(0\le d\le M\).
\item \textbf{(Weights.)} Each \(\beta_i\) is strictly positive and
\[
\beta=\left(\frac{1}{2(m-1)},1,1\right),
\]
so in particular \(\beta_y=\beta_z=1\).
\item \textbf{(Robustness.)} \(\kappa\ge 0\).
\end{itemize}
Write
\[
K_d=\{t\in\Delta_M:t_y+t_z\ge d\},
\qquad
\Phi(t)=\sum_i\alpha_i t_i+\kappa\sqrt{\sum_i\beta_i t_i^2}.
\]
For \(\kappa>0\), for every nonempty active support \(S\subseteq\{x,y,z\}\) and multiplier \(\lambda\) satisfying
\[
\sum_{i\in S}\frac{(\lambda-\alpha_i)^2}{\beta_i}=\kappa^2,\qquad
\alpha_i<\lambda\ \text{for }i\in S,\qquad
\lambda\le \alpha_j\ \text{for }j\notin S,
\]
let \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) be the active-set point
\[
t^{\mathrm{rel}}_i=
\begin{cases}
\displaystyle
\frac{M((\lambda-\alpha_i)/\beta_i)}
{\sum_{h\in S}(\lambda-\alpha_h)/\beta_h}, & i\in S,\\[1em]
0, & i\notin S .
\end{cases}
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\in K_d\), then
\[
\Phi(t^{\mathrm{rel}})\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d.
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}_y+t^{\mathrm{rel}}_z<d\), then
\[
t^\star=(M-d,s^\star,d-s^\star)\in K_d
\]
and
\[
\Phi(t^\star)\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d,
\]
where \(s^\star=\operatorname{truncSelector}(M,d,\alpha,\beta,\kappa)\), namely with
\[
\delta=\alpha_y-\alpha_z,\qquad A=\beta_x(M-d)^2,
\]
\[
s^\star=
\begin{cases}
0, & \displaystyle \delta\ge \frac{\kappa d}{\sqrt{A+d^2}},\\[0.8em]
d, & \displaystyle \delta\le -\frac{\kappa d}{\sqrt{A+d^2}},\\[0.8em]
\displaystyle
\frac{d-\delta\sqrt{(A+d^2/2)/(\kappa^2-\delta^2/2)}}{2},
& \text{otherwise.}
\end{cases}
\]

For \(\kappa=0\), let \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) be a point of the exposed \(\alpha\)-minimizing face
\[
\{t\in\Delta_M:\ t_i\ne 0\Rightarrow \alpha_i\le \alpha_j\ \text{for every }j\}.
\]
Assume the tie convention that, if this exposed face contains at least one point of \(K_d\), then the selected point \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) lies in \(K_d\). If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\in K_d\), then
\[
\Phi(t^{\mathrm{rel}})\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d.
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}_y+t^{\mathrm{rel}}_z<d\), then
\[
t^\star=(M-d,s^\star,d-s^\star)\in K_d
\]
and
\[
\Phi(t^\star)\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d,
\]
where \(s^\star=\operatorname{truncSelector}(M,d,\alpha,\beta,0)\), equivalently, in the ordered selector convention, \(s^\star=0\) if \(\alpha_y-\alpha_z\ge 0\) and \(s^\star=d\) if \(\alpha_y-\alpha_z<0\).

nl.statement: Let q=2(m-1), T_m={x,y,z>=0: qx+y+z=2m}, phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z)=c_x x+c_y y+c_z z+kappa sqrt(qx^2+y^2+z^2), and d_m=0 if m is even and d_m=2/m if m is odd. Then Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)=min_{(x,y,z) in T_m, y+z>=d_m} phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z)-min_{(x,y,z) in T_m} phi_{r,kappa}(x,y,z).
